ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "uuid" TEXT /* Unique string identifying the connected client */,
  "owner_uuid" TEXT /* Unique string identifying the server member */,
  "is_owner_connection" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the connection is to the owner member */,
  "client_type" INTEGER /* Type of the client (e.g., JAVA, CPP, CSHARP) */,
  "serialization_version" INTEGER /* Client-side supported serialization version */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client */,
  "last_connection_time" TEXT /* Last time the client connected */,
  "last_disconnection_time" TEXT /* Last time the client disconnected */,
  "connection_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of connection attempts */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the client is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the client record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the client record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clients_owner_uuid FOREIGN KEY ("owner_uuid") REFERENCES server_members ("owner_uuid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clients_serialization_version FOREIGN KEY ("serialization_version") REFERENCES serialization_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clients_client_type FOREIGN KEY ("client_type") REFERENCES client_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "password_salt" TEXT /* Salt used in password hashing */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the user has administrative privileges */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "session_start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "session_end_time" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user for session tracking */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for session tracking */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the session is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client associated with the connection */,
  "connection_time" TEXT /* Time the client connected */,
  "disconnection_time" TEXT /* Time the client disconnected */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client for connection tracking */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the connection */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session associated with the connection */,
  "is_secure" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the connection is secure */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the connection record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the connection record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_connections_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_connections_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES user_sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_connections_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "server_members" (
  "member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server member */,
  "owner_uuid" TEXT /* Unique string identifying the server member */,
  "member_name" TEXT /* Name of the server member */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server member */,
  "last_heartbeat" TEXT /* Last time the server member sent a heartbeat */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the server member (e.g., online, offline, maintenance) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the server member record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the server member record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("member_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the client type (e.g., JAVA, CPP, CSHARP) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the client type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "serialization_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each serialization version */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Client-side supported serialization version */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the serialization version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_type_versions" (
  "type_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client type and version relationship */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client type */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the serialization version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_type_versions_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES serialization_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_client_type_versions_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES client_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_controls" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level granted to the user (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  "is_allowed" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the access is currently allowed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access control entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access control entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_controls_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_controls_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, logout, file access) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the action */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the client or user for audit purposes */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for audit purposes */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the action was successful */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the audit log entry was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "message" TEXT /* Notification message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the notification has been read */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration setting */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the configuration setting is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)
